title: Represent Dates, Contacts, and Quoted Sources
summary: Build and verify semantic time address quotes in public advisory provenance desk through prediction, parsed-structure inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain semantic time address quotes from purpose and parsed structure.
Apply semantic time address quotes while retaining prior HTML requirements.
Diagnose the named misconception in a changed content and interaction scenario.

options.text: Public advisory provenance desk case 1: address is the correct wrapper for any street location, even when it is not contact information for the page or article.
Public advisory provenance desk case 1: use time, address, quotation, and citation elements only when their defined relationship matches the content.
Public advisory provenance desk case 1: put every date in a generic span because machines infer dates from visible punctuation.

steps.title: Read the public advisory provenance desk relationship · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 2: Which claim preserves semantic time address quotes when the styling and content change? Scenario: public advisory provenance desk.
steps.why: Represent Dates, Contacts, and Quoted Sources keeps meaning testable instead of depending on appearance. This is checkpoint 2 in public advisory provenance desk.

content.text: Time connects human-readable dates to machine-readable datetime values. Address contains contact information for the nearest article or page author or owner, not every location mentioned in prose. Blockquote and q identify quoted material, while cite identifies the source work or title rather than a person by default. Apply it to public advisory provenance desk.
